summary: Genesis Project Reset: Task Registration & Harness

message.content:
verifique os arquivos na pasta ./genesis, leia-os, entenda-os e inicie a tarefa definida em MMS_V1_GENESIS.md - avalie também o status
  dos arquivos (git), pois estavamos em uma tentativa anterior com outro LLM que falhou, siga estritamente as instrucoes e primeiro
  reinicie o harness, como especificado, comite a cada pequeno progresso e siga indefinidamente até a completa conclusão do plano.
  IMPORTANTE: Todas as instrucoes foram definidas de forma premeditada e devem ser completamente seguidas, NUNCA considere nada como
  opcional ou aceite deixar coisas para ser resolvidas depois, complete 100% de cada pequena tarefa, cada pequena tarefa deve ser entregue
  com qualidade produtiva, sem mocks, sem "FIXME", sem "TODO"s, os unicos "TODO"s são os que serão definidos no harness após o
  planejamento e as possíveis novas tarefas decorrentes das primeiras, sucessivamente. ultrathink!!! siga até a conclusão! Lembre-se,
  primeira coisa, registrar as tarefas no harness, independente do que dizem os documentos, deve ser seguido o harness, tudo
    deve ser registrado no harness, seja por subagentes ou por você (atualize os docs do "genesis" para refletir isso), primeira coisa,
  faca backup do
    harness e reinicie com as tarefas de "genesis" (reset v1, consolidacao de anos de conhecimento). Atualize os docs para refletir isso:
    Tudo deve ser feito apenas após registro no harness novo, comitar a cada pequeno progresso, registrar no progress.txt tudo de modo que
    em caso de erro ou de estouro de contexto, possamos reiniciar sem nenhum tipo de perca.
  Resete o projeto, corrija o CLAUDE.md, atualize os arquivos do harness, siga as instrucoes
 - O unico arquivo 100% confiavel é o RFQ, pois foi enviado pelo cliente, o restante tudo pode ter alucinacao de AI.

content.content:
1→(Este arquivo tem pequenos erros, mas deve ser considerado, porem verificar com cuidado, ele menciona coisas como usar nomes em portugues, sendo que o projeto é para o metro de Dubai.)
     2→
     3→# MMS V1.0 GENESIS — Master Orchestration Architecture
     4→
     5→> **Nature**: Orchestration architecture document. Teaches concepts, defines phases, delegates work.
     6→> **Principle**: Instill heuristics, not rigid rules. Quality over economy.
     7→> **Resources**: UNLIMITED agents and tokens. Never economize.
     8→
     9→---
    10→
    11→## PART I: SYSTEM IDENTITY
    12→
    13→### 1.1 Role Definition
    14→
    15→You are the **Lead System Architect & Knowledge Orchestrator** for MMS (Metro Mileage Management System).
    16→
    17→You operate as an **orchestration operating system**:
    18→- Manage context as a finite resource with diminishing returns
    19→- Delegate to specialized sub-agents with clean context windows
    20→- Maintain state across sessions through disk-based persistence
    21→- Return condensed summaries (1,000-2,000 tokens) from sub-agents
    22→
    23→### 1.2 Core Behavioral Heuristics
    24→
    25→These are judgment principles, not step-by-step rules:
    26→
    27→```
    28→HEURISTIC 1: Trust Nothing, Verify Everything
    29→- Documentation lies. Code may not run. Tests may not pass.
    30→- Every claim requires evidence: file:line, commit:hash, or execution proof.
    31→- Categorize: VERIFIED (ran) | CLAIMED (documented) | DEPRECATED (ignore)
    32→
    33→HEURISTIC 2: Parallel Wide, Sequential Deep
    34→- Extraction: Many agents, maximum coverage, loose coordination
    35→- Validation: Cross-reference, resolve conflicts, identify gaps
    36→- Synthesis: Single agent, full context, coherent output
    37→
    38→HEURISTIC 3: Disk Over Memory
    39→- Context windows rot. Write everything to _migration_memory/
    40→- Sub-agents explore extensively (tens of thousands of tokens)
    41→- Return only condensed findings to orchestrator
    42→
    43→HEURISTIC 4: One Feature, One Session
    44→- Never "boil the ocean" — incremental progress compounds
    45→- Verify previous work before starting new work
    46→- Leave codebase in mergeable state at session end
    47→
    48→HEURISTIC 5: Decisions Through Debate
    49→- Spawn adversarial agents for major decisions
    50→- SIMPLICITY advocate vs COMPLETENESS advocate vs PERFORMANCE advocate
    51→- Synthesizer produces consensus from position papers
    52→```
    53→
    54→---
    55→
    56→## PART II: PROJECT CONTEXT
    57→
    58→### 2.1 Product Definition
    59→
    60→**MMS (Metro Mileage Management System) V1.0**
    61→
    62→A high-performance visualization system for railway fleet maintenance planning.
    63→
    64→| Attribute | Specification |
    65→|-----------|---------------|
    66→| UX Pattern | Semantic Zoom (Google Earth-style) with 3 LOD levels |
    67→| Performance Target | 60fps rendering 50+ trains, 10 years of data |
    68→| Primary Metric | Km Balancing (fleet mileage equalization) |
    69→| Differentiators | Transparent justifications (3+ reasons per recommendation), What-If simulation, unified maintenance-operations interface |
    70→
    71→### 2.2 Regulatory Domain
    72→
    73→| Standard | Scope | Key Requirements |
    74→|----------|-------|------------------|
    75→| ECM 2019/779 | Entity in Charge of Maintenance | F1: Management, F2: Development, F3: Fleet Management, F4: Delivery |
    76→| EN 50126-1 | RAMS | Reliability (MTBF), Availability (%), Maintainability (MTTR), Safety |
    77→| EN 15380 | Terminology | A-Check/PM1, B-Check/PM2, C-Check/HM, D-Overhaul/OVH |
    78→
    79→### 2.3 Current State (6 Knowledge Nodes)
    80→
    81→```
    82→~/antonio/mms/
    83→├── system/          # PRIMARY: React 18 + R3F + BlueprintJS 6
    84→│   ├── apps/canvas/ # Semantic canvas with LOD layers
    85→│   ├── docs/        # MMS_COMPLETE_SPECIFICATION.md (8922 lines)
    86→│   ├── packages/    # Local toolkit (DEPRECATED)
    87→│   └── .harness/    # Session history (81KB progress.txt)
    88→├── deck/            # POC: deck.gl approach, harness research
    89→├── MFC/             # Full-stack: Next.js 15 + Python OR-Tools solver
    90→├── projects/        # Historical: 4 subdirectories of past attempts
    91→├── design/          # Visual: 9 high-resolution reference images
    92→└── system_started/  # Template: Bootstrap monorepo structure
    93→```
    94→
    95→### 2.4 Target State
    96→
    97→```
    98→~/antonio/mms/system/  (V1.0 Clean Baseline)
    99→├── apps/canvas/       # PatternFly v6 + WebGL Canvas
   100→├── docs/
   101→│   └── SPECIFICATION.md  # Single source of truth (~3000-4000 lines)
   102→├── .harness/          # V1.0 harness (features.json, progress.txt)
   103→└── CLAUDE.md          # Updated for V1.0
   104→```
   105→
   106→---
   107→
   108→## PART III: IMMUTABLE CONSTRAINTS
   109→
   110→These are **physics laws** — cannot be violated under any circumstance:
   111→
   112→| Legacy | V1.0 | Rule |
   113→|--------|------|------|
   114→| BlueprintJS | PatternFly v6 | All UI components must use PatternFly |
   115→| @zazcart/toolkit | @belt/tools | All state management via npm @belt/tools |
   116→| packages/toolkit/ | DELETE | Local code replaced by public npm |
   117→| Portuguese docs | English | All documentation in English |
   118→| 8922-line spec | ~3000-4000 lines | Consolidate, don't expand |
   119→| React 18.3.1 | React 18.3.1 | Locked due to dependency constraints |
   120→
   121→---
   122→
   123→## PART IV: EXECUTION PHASES
   124→
   125→### Phase 1: Territory Mapping
   126→
   127→**Objective**: Understand landscape before extraction.
   128→**Agents**: 1
   129→**Output**: `_migration_memory/00_TERRITORY_MAP.md`
   130→
   131→```
   132→AGENT INSTRUCTION:
   133→Scan all 6 nodes in ~/antonio/mms/. For each:
   134→1. Count files by type (.tsx, .ts, .py, .md, .json)
   135→2. Identify key files (specs, solvers, components, configs)
   136→3. Check git history (commit count, date range, milestones)
   137→4. Note any README or CLAUDE.md with context
   138→
   139→DO NOT read file contents. Map territory only.
   140→Write findings to _migration_memory/00_TERRITORY_MAP.md
   141→```
   142→
   143→---
   144→
   145→### Phase 2: Parallel Knowledge Extraction
   146→
   147→**Objective**: Extract all unique knowledge from all nodes.
   148→**Agents**: 8-10 parallel
   149→**Protocol**: Hypothesis-Evidence-Verification-Artifact (HEVA)
   150→
   151→| Agent ID | Target | Output |
   152→|----------|--------|--------|
   153→| EXT-CANVAS | system/apps/canvas/ | 01_canvas.md |
   154→| EXT-DOCS | system/docs/ | 02_domain.md |
   155→| EXT-HARNESS | system/.harness/ + git log | 03_decisions.md |
   156→| EXT-MFC | MFC/ (solver, math, methodology) | 04_solver.md |
   157→| EXT-DECK | deck/ (POCs, harness research) | 05_visualization.md |
   158→| EXT-PROJECTS | projects/ | 06_experiments.md |
   159→| EXT-DESIGN | design/ images | 07_visual_spec.md |
   160→| EXT-MARKET | All repos for competitor mentions | 08_market.md |
   161→
   162→```
   163→AGENT INSTRUCTION TEMPLATE:
   164→You are extracting knowledge from [TARGET].
   165→
   166→PROTOCOL (HEVA):
   167→For each finding:
   168→1. HYPOTHESIS: "I believe [X] because..."
   169→2. EVIDENCE: "Found in [file:line] or [commit:hash]..."
   170→3. VERIFICATION: "Confirmed by [code executed / test passed / screenshot]..."
   171→4. ARTIFACT: Written to _migration_memory/[OUTPUT_FILE]
   172→
   173→CATEGORIZATION:
   174→- VERIFIED: Code ran, test passed, screenshot captured
   175→- CLAIMED: Documentation says it works, not verified
   176→- UNIQUE_KNOWLEDGE: Insights, formulas, patterns worth preserving
   177→- DEPRECATED: Should not be carried forward
   178→
   179→BE THOROUGH. We have unlimited tokens. Miss nothing.
   180→```
   181→
   182→---
   183→
   184→### Phase 3: Technology Research
   185→
   186→**Objective**: Deep research on new technologies to adopt.
   187→**Agents**: 3 parallel
   188→**Timing**: AFTER extraction, not before
   189→
   190→| Agent ID | Focus | Output |
   191→|----------|-------|--------|
   192→| TECH-PF | PatternFly v6 deep dive | 10_patternfly.md |
   193→| TECH-BELT | @belt/tools API analysis | 11_belt_tools.md |
   194→| TECH-CANVAS | Canvas technology comparison | 12_canvas_tech.md |
   195→
   196→```
   197→TECH-PF INSTRUCTION:
   198→Research PatternFly v6 comprehensively.
   199→- Official docs: https://www.patternfly.org/
   200→- GitHub: https://github.com/patternfly/patternfly-react
   201→- Focus: Topology view, Table, Charts, Drawer, Layout patterns
   202→- Document: WebGL canvas integration within PatternFly app
   203→- Document: Component mapping from BlueprintJS
   204→- Document: Design token migration (--pf-v5-* → --pf-t--*)
   205→- Document: Codemods available (npx @patternfly/pf-codemods)
   206→
   207→TECH-BELT INSTRUCTION:
   208→Analyze @belt/tools npm package.
   209→- Execute: npm view @belt/tools
   210→- Read actual source code or type definitions
   211→- Document: createSlice, createAsyncSlice, createMachine APIs
   212→- Document: Cache policies, trackMetadata option
   213→- Document: Migration path from system/packages/toolkit/
   214→- If package doesn't exist publicly, document what's needed
   215→
   216→TECH-CANVAS INSTRUCTION:
   217→Compare canvas technologies with benchmarks.
   218→- Evaluate: React Three Fiber, deck.gl, PixiJS, Konva, raw Three.js
   219→- Criteria: 60fps with 60k entities, PatternFly integration, DX, bundle size
   220→- Run actual benchmarks if possible
   221→- Document recommendation with evidence
   222→- Consider: Current R3F implementation in system/ may be sufficient
   223→```
   224→
   225→---
   226→
   227→### Phase 4: Cross-Validation
   228→
   229→**Objective**: Verify findings across sources, resolve conflicts, identify gaps.
   230→**Agents**: 4 parallel
   231→
   232→| Agent ID | Focus | Output |
   233→|----------|-------|--------|
   234→| VAL-MATH | Mathematical formula consistency | 20_math_validation.md |
   235→| VAL-DOMAIN | Domain terminology correctness | 21_domain_validation.md |
   236→| VAL-TECH | Technical claim verification | 22_tech_validation.md |
   237→| VAL-PERF | Performance claim verification | 23_perf_validation.md |
   238→
   239→```
   240→VALIDATION AGENT INSTRUCTION:
   241→You are validating findings from Phase 2.
   242→
   243→Read extraction artifacts in _migration_memory/*.md.
   244→Cross-reference claims between different sources.
   245→
   246→Identify:
   247→- CONFLICTS: Different sources say different things
   248→- GAPS: Knowledge that should exist but doesn't
   249→- VERIFIED: Multiple sources agree AND code confirms
   250→
   251→For conflicts: Document both sides with evidence.
   252→For gaps: Note what's missing and where it should come from.
   253→
   254→Write to _migration_memory/[OUTPUT_FILE]
   255→```
   256→
   257→---
   258→
   259→### Phase 5: Decision Debates
   260→
   261→**Objective**: Make final decisions on technology and architecture.
   262→**Agents**: 4 in structured debate
   263→**Pattern**: Adversarial position papers → Cross-examination → Synthesis
   264→
   265→**Debate Topics**:
   266→1. Canvas technology choice (R3F vs alternatives)
   267→2. Specification structure (single file vs modular)
   268→3. V1.0 scope (canvas-only vs full system)
   269→4. PatternFly integration approach
   270→
   271→```
   272→ROUND 1: Position Papers (parallel)
   273→- DEB-SIMPLE: Argue for simplest approach that could work
   274→- DEB-COMPLETE: Argue for most comprehensive approach
   275→- DEB-PERF: Argue for performance-first approach
   276→- Each writes 500-word position to _migration_memory/30_debate_[perspective].md
   277→
   278→ROUND 2: Cross-Examination (sequential)
   279→- Each agent reads other positions
   280→- Writes refutation/agreement with evidence
   281→
   282→ROUND 3: Synthesis (single agent)
   283→- DEB-SYNTH: Reads all positions and refutations
   284→- Produces consensus document: _migration_memory/35_decisions.md
   285→- Must justify final decision with evidence from debate
   286→```
   287→
   288→---
   289→
   290→### Phase 6: Specification Writing
   291→
   292→**Objective**: Produce final `docs/SPECIFICATION.md`.
   293→**Agents**: 3 writers parallel + 1 editor sequential
   294→
   295→| Agent ID | Sections |
   296→|----------|----------|
   297→| SPEC-DOMAIN | Part I: Domain (ECM, RAMS, Railway terminology, Math formulas) |
   298→| SPEC-ARCH | Part II: Architecture (Canvas, State, API, Data structures) |
   299→| SPEC-UX | Part III: UX (PatternFly components, Design system, Demo strategy) |
   300→| SPEC-EDIT | Final pass: Consistency, formatting, deduplication |
   301→
   302→```
   303→SPECIFICATION WRITER INSTRUCTION:
   304→You are writing the definitive MMS V1.0 specification.
   305→
   306→INPUTS (read first):
   307→- All files in _migration_memory/
   308→- Decision document from Phase 5 (35_decisions.md)
   309→
   310→RULES:
   311→1. This is a FINAL document, not a migration plan
   312→2. No historical notes ("we used to...", "previously...")
   313→3. No alternatives ("you could also...") — only the decided approach
   314→4. Include ALL validated mathematical formulas
   315→5. Include ALL domain knowledge (ECM, RAMS, EN 15380)
   316→6. Include PatternFly component mappings with code examples
   317→7. Include @belt/tools patterns with code examples
   318→8. Include demo strategy and "wow moments"
   319→9. Write in clear, technical English
   320→10. Target ~3000-4000 lines total
   321→
   322→Write your assigned sections to docs/SPECIFICATION.md
   323→```
   324→
   325→---
   326→
   327→### Phase 7: Repository Cleanup
   328→
   329→**Objective**: Transform codebase into V1.0 clean baseline.
   330→**Agent**: 1 (requires careful sequencing)
   331→
   332→```bash
   333→# 1. Create backup branch
   334→git checkout -b backup/pre-v1-$(date +%Y%m%d)
   335→git push origin backup/pre-v1-$(date +%Y%m%d)
   336→
   337→# 2. Return to main
   338→git checkout main
   339→
   340→# 3. Remove deprecated directories
   341→rm -rf packages/
   342→rm -rf agent_docs/
   343→
   344→# 4. Clean docs (preserve only new SPECIFICATION.md)
   345→find docs/ -name "*.md" ! -name "SPECIFICATION.md" -delete
   346→
   347→# 5. Update dependencies in package.json
   348→# - Remove @zazcart/toolkit references
   349→# - Add @belt/tools
   350→# - Add @patternfly/react-core@6.x
   351→# - Add @patternfly/react-table@6.x
   352→# - Add @patternfly/react-topology@6.x
   353→
   354→# 6. Archive old harness, create new
   355→mkdir -p .harness/archive/pre-v1
   356→mv .harness/*.txt .harness/archive/pre-v1/
   357→mv .harness/*.json .harness/archive/pre-v1/
   358→
   359→# 7. Create new harness files (see Phase 8 templates)
   360→
   361→# 8. Update CLAUDE.md for V1.0
   362→
   363→# 9. Delete migration memory (git preserves history)
   364→rm -rf _migration_memory/
   365→
   366→# 10. Commit clean baseline
   367→git add -A
   368→git commit -m "chore: MMS V1.0 genesis - clean baseline
   369→
   370→- Removed deprecated packages/ directory
   371→- Consolidated documentation to SPECIFICATION.md
   372→- Updated dependencies for PatternFly v6 + @belt/tools
   373→- Reset harness for V1.0 feature development
   374→- Archived pre-V1 harness history"
   375→```
   376→
   377→---
   378→
   379→### Phase 8: Implementation
   380→
   381→**Objective**: Build V1.0 features following harness protocol.
   382→**Pattern**: Anthropic's two-agent architecture (Initializer + Coding Agent)
   383→
   384→#### 8.1 Harness Files (JSON for structure integrity)
   385→
   386→**features.json** (created by Initializer Agent):
   387→```json
   388→{
   389→  "version": "1.0.0",
   390→  "created": "2025-01-XX",
   391→  "features": [
   392→    {
   393→      "id": "F001",
   394→      "category": "foundation",
   395→      "description": "Vite + React 18 + PatternFly v6 scaffold",
   396→      "verification": [
   397→        "npm run dev starts without errors",
   398→        "PatternFly components render correctly",
   399→        "No console errors in browser"
   400→      ],
   401→      "passes": false,
   402→      "priority": 1
   403→    },
   404→    {
   405→      "id": "F002",
   406→      "category": "foundation",
   407→      "description": "Storybook 8 setup with PatternFly theme",
   408→      "verification": [
   409→        "npm run storybook starts",
   410→        "Sample story renders PatternFly button"
   411→      ],
   412→      "passes": false,
   413→      "priority": 2
   414→    },
   415→    {
   416→      "id": "F003",
   417→      "category": "foundation",
   418→      "description": "@belt/tools integration",
   419→      "verification": [
   420→        "createSlice works in test file",
   421→        "State updates trigger re-renders"
   422→      ],
   423→      "passes": false,
   424→      "priority": 3
   425→    }
   426→  ]
   427→}
   428→```
   429→
   430→**Feature List (complete)**:
   431→```
   432→PHASE A: Canvas Foundation
   433→F001: Vite + React 18 + PatternFly v6 scaffold
   434→F002: Storybook 8 setup
   435→F003: @belt/tools integration
   436→F004: Canvas container component (accepts data props)
   437→F005: Macro LOD layer (heatmap, single draw call)
   438→F006: Meso LOD layer (task blocks, InstancedMesh)
   439→F007: Micro LOD layer (detail cards, HTML overlay)
   440→F008: LOD transitions (crossfade 0.1 zoom units)
   441→F009: Pan/Zoom controls
   442→F010: 60fps verification (performance test)
   443→
   444→PHASE B: UI Integration
   445→F011: Train sidebar (PatternFly Table)
   446→F012: Timeline header
   447→F013: Minimap
   448→F014: Task details (PatternFly Drawer)
   449→F015: Keyboard navigation
   450→
   451→PHASE C: Solver Integration
   452→F016: Mock API with realistic data
   453→F017: Real solver connection
   454→F018: Allocation workflow UI
   455→F019: What-If simulation
   456→F020: Demo mode with scripted scenarios
   457→```
   458→
   459→#### 8.2 Session Protocol (Coding Agent)
   460→
   461→```
   462→EVERY SESSION:
   463→
   464→1. RECOVER CONTEXT
   465→   cat .harness/progress.txt | tail -100
   466→   git log --oneline -15
   467→   cat .harness/features.json | jq '.features[] | select(.passes==false) | .id' | head -1
   468→
   469→2. VERIFY PREVIOUS
   470→   Run the app
   471→   Confirm last completed feature still works
   472→   If broken: fix before proceeding
   473→
   474→3. SELECT ONE FEATURE
   475→   Pick the lowest-priority incomplete feature
   476→   Read its verification criteria
   477→
   478→4. IMPLEMENT
   479→   Write code for ONE feature only
   480→   Follow patterns in SPECIFICATION.md
   481→   Use PatternFly components, @belt/tools state
   482→
   483→5. VERIFY
   484→   Run all verification steps for the feature
   485→   Take screenshot if visual
   486→   Run tests if applicable
   487→
   488→6. COMMIT
   489→   git add -A
   490→   git commit -m "feat(F0XX): [description]
   491→
   492→   Verification:
   493→   - [x] Step 1 passed
   494→   - [x] Step 2 passed"
   495→
   496→7. UPDATE HARNESS
   497→   Mark feature as passes: true in features.json
   498→   Append session summary to progress.txt:
   499→   - What was accomplished
   500→   - Decisions made and rationale
   501→   - What should happen next
   502→   - Any blockers or concerns
   503→
   504→8. STOP
   505→   Leave codebase in clean, mergeable state
   506→   Do not start another feature
   507→```
   508→
   509→---
   510→
   511→## PART V: PRESERVED KNOWLEDGE
   512→
   513→### 5.1 Critical Technical Learnings
   514→
   515→**STATE-ARCH-001: TypedArrays + SoA for 60fps**
   516→```javascript
   517→// Structure of Arrays pattern
   518→const trainData = {
   519→  ids: new Uint32Array(MAX_TRAINS),
   520→  x: new Float32Array(MAX_TRAINS),
   521→  y: new Float32Array(MAX_TRAINS),
   522→  km: new Float32Array(MAX_TRAINS),
   523→  status: new Uint8Array(MAX_TRAINS),
   524→  count: 0
   525→};
   526→
   527→// Update without GC pressure
   528→function updateTrain(index, x, y, km) {
   529→  trainData.x[index] = x;
   530→  trainData.y[index] = y;
   531→  trainData.km[index] = km;
   532→  // No object allocation = no GC = stable 60fps
   533→}
   534→```
   535→
   536→**TOOLKIT-PERF-001: 7 GC Optimization Fixes**
   537→1. Copy array before iteration (unsafe unsubscribe)
   538→2. Skip validation in production (hot path)
   539→3. Object.assign instead of reduceObject (O(N) vs O(N²))
   540→4. `trackMetadata: false` option (eliminate GC thrashing)
   541→5. Clean object structure (prevent V8 de-optimization)
   542→6. Direct assignment (avoid double proxying)
   543→7. Safe error handling in devLog
   544→
   545→**LOD System Thresholds**:
   546→```javascript
   547→const LOD_THRESHOLDS = {
   548→  MACRO: { max: 0.5 },   // zoom < 0.5x: Aggregated heatmap
   549→  MESO: { min: 0.5, max: 2.0 },  // 0.5x-2x: Task blocks
   550→  MICRO: { min: 2.0 }    // zoom > 2x: Detail cards
   551→};
   552→
   553→const CROSSFADE_WIDTH = 0.1; // Smooth transition zone
   554→```
   555→
   556→**RBush Spatial Index**: O(log n) viewport culling
   557→```javascript
   558→import RBush from 'rbush';
   559→const tree = new RBush();
   560→tree.load(tasks.map(t => ({
   561→  minX: t.x, minY: t.y,
   562→  maxX: t.x + t.width, maxY: t.y + t.height,
   563→  task: t
   564→})));
   565→const visible = tree.search(viewport);
   566→```
   567→
   568→### 5.2 Domain Knowledge
   569→
   570→**ECM 2019/779 Functions**:
   571→| Function | Name | MMS Feature |
   572→|----------|------|-------------|
   573→| F1 | Management | Audit logs, compliance dashboard |
   574→| F2 | Maintenance Development | Rule configuration, threshold settings |
   575→| F3 | Fleet Maintenance Management | Km balancing, scheduling optimization |
   576→| F4 | Maintenance Delivery | Work order tracking, completion status |
   577→
   578→**RAMS Metrics (EN 50126-1)**:
   579→```javascript
   580→const RAMS = {
   581→  reliability: {
   582→    mtbf: totalOperatingHours / failureCount,
   583→    failureRate: failureCount / totalOperatingHours
   584→  },
   585→  availability: {
   586→    operational: (totalTime - downtimeHours) / totalTime * 100,
   587→    inherent: mtbf / (mtbf + mttr)
   588→  },
   589→  maintainability: {
   590→    mttr: totalRepairHours / repairCount,
   591→    completionRate: completedWorkOrders / totalWorkOrders * 100
   592→  },
   593→  safety: {
   594→    hazardRate: safetyIncidents / operatingHours,
   595→    severityIndex: weightedSeveritySum / incidentCount
   596→  }
   597→};
   598→```
   599→
   600→**EN 15380 Terminology Mapping**:
   601→| Common Term | EN 15380 Code | Interval | Duration |
   602→|-------------|---------------|----------|----------|
   603→| A-Check | PM1 | 15,000 km | 15-60 min |
   604→| B-Check | PM2 | 45,000 km | 4-16 hours |
   605→| C-Check | HM | 90,000 km | 1-3 days |
   606→| D-Overhaul | OVH | 500,000-800,000 km | 2-6 weeks |
   607→
   608→**KPI Formulas**:
   609→```javascript
   610→// Km Variance (coefficient of variation) - PRIMARY METRIC
   611→const kmVariance = (standardDeviation(currentKms) / mean(currentKms)) * 100;
   612→
   613→// Fleet Availability
   614→const availability = (fitTrains / totalTrains) * 100;
   615→
   616→// Depot Utilization
   617→const utilization = (scheduledTracks / availableTracks) * 100;
   618→
   619→// Train Delta (individual vs fleet average)
   620→const trainDelta = ((trainKm - fleetAvgKm) / fleetAvgKm) * 100;
   621→
   622→// Maintenance Window Efficiency
   623→const windowEfficiency = actualWorkHours / scheduledWindowHours * 100;
   624→```
   625→
   626→### 5.3 Competitive Analysis
   627→
   628→| Competitor | Focus | Strength | MMS Opportunity |
   629→|------------|-------|----------|-----------------|
   630→| DELMIA Quintiq | Large-scale optimization | Powerful solver | Simpler implementation, faster time-to-value |
   631→| Tracsis | UK rail operations | Strong timetabling | Less maintenance focus |
   632→| Railnova | Fleet telematics | Real-time data, 96% ML accuracy | Basic optimization only |
   633→| SignatureRail | Fleet maintenance | Clean UI, good workflow | No optimization, black-box decisions |
   634→| ZEDAS | ECM compliance | AR maintenance assistance | Complex, enterprise-only |
   635→
   636→**MMS Differentiators**:
   637→1. Km Balancing as primary objective (competitors treat as secondary)
   638→2. Transparent justifications (3+ reasons per recommendation)
   639→3. What-If simulation accessible to non-technical users
   640→4. Unified maintenance ↔ operations interface
   641→
   642→### 5.4 Demo Strategy
   643→
   644→**Timeline**: 12 minutes total
   645→
   646→| Time | Moment | Action | Wow Factor |
   647→|------|--------|--------|------------|
   648→| 0:30-2:00 | Variance Discovery | Dashboard shows 18% km variance | "Your fleet is out of balance" |
   649→| 2:00-5:00 | Justification Card | Click recommendation, see 3+ reasons | "Transparent AI decisions" |
   650→| 5:00-8:00 | What-If Simulation | Drag task, see instant projection | "<10s response time" |
   651→| 8:00-11:00 | Map Context | Toggle map view, see km delta overlay | "Geographic intelligence" |
   652→| 11:00-12:00 | Close | Summary metrics, next steps | "Reduce variance to 8%" |
   653→
   654→**Credibility Elements**:
   655→- EN 15380 terminology throughout
   656→- Real Portuguese depot names (Entroncamento, Campolide, Santa Apolónia)
   657→- Realistic service codes (PM1-2024-0847)
   658→- Plausible km ranges (45,000 - 180,000 km)
   659→
   660→---
   661→
   662→## PART VI: SPECIAL COMMANDS
   663→
   664→### ULTRATHINK
   665→
   666→When user says "ULTRATHINK":
   667→1. Stop immediately
   668→2. Review last 3 steps recursively
   669→3. Check for hallucination or assumption
   670→4. Document verification process
   671→5. Only continue after explicit confirmation
   672→
   673→### ESCALATE
   674→
   675→Escalate to user ONLY if:
   676→- Decision requires business input (not technical)
   677→- Irreconcilable conflict between sources
   678→- Blocker preventing progress
   679→- Discovery that significantly changes scope
   680→
   681→For everything else: research, decide, document rationale.
   682→
   683→### CHECKPOINT
   684→
   685→When user says "CHECKPOINT":
   686→1. Write current state to _migration_memory/CHECKPOINT_[timestamp].md
   687→2. Summarize: completed phases, pending phases, key decisions, blockers
   688→3. Commit all changes with checkpoint message
   689→4. Provide recovery instructions for next session
   690→
   691→---
   692→
   693→## PART VII: EXECUTION CHECKLIST
   694→
   695→```
   696→SESSION 1: Phases 1-2 (Territory + Extraction)
   697→[ ] mkdir -p ~/antonio/mms/system/_migration_memory
   698→[ ] Run Phase 1: Territory mapping (1 agent)
   699→[ ] Run Phase 2: Knowledge extraction (8-10 agents parallel)
   700→[ ] Verify all artifacts written to disk
   701→[ ] Review for completeness before proceeding
   702→
   703→SESSION 2: Phases 3-4 (Research + Validation)
   704→[ ] Run Phase 3: Technology research (3 agents parallel)
   705→[ ] Run Phase 4: Cross-validation (4 agents parallel)
   706→[ ] Review conflicts and gaps
   707→[ ] Document unresolved issues
   708→
   709→SESSION 3: Phase 5 (Debates)
   710→[ ] Run Phase 5: Decision debates (4 agents)
   711→[ ] Produce consensus document
   712→[ ] Verify all major decisions made
   713→[ ] No open questions remaining
   714→
   715→SESSION 4: Phase 6 (Specification)
   716→[ ] Run Phase 6: Specification writing (4 agents)
   717→[ ] Review and edit final document
   718→[ ] Verify completeness against checklist
   719→[ ] Document ready for implementation
   720→
   721→SESSION 5: Phase 7 (Cleanup)
   722→[ ] Run Phase 7: Repository cleanup
   723→[ ] Verify clean codebase
   724→[ ] Delete _migration_memory/
   725→[ ] Commit V1.0 baseline
   726→
   727→SESSION 6+: Phase 8 (Implementation)
   728→[ ] Follow harness protocol
   729→[ ] One feature per session
   730→[ ] Verify before commit
   731→[ ] Update progress.txt
   732→```
   733→
   734→---
   735→
   736→## IMMEDIATE START
   737→
   738→Upon receiving this prompt:
   739→
   740→```bash
   741→# 1. Create memory directory
   742→mkdir -p ~/antonio/mms/system/_migration_memory
   743→
   744→# 2. Start Phase 1

   746→
   747→# 3. Parallel: Start Phase 2

   753→# 5. End of session

   756→
   757→**Do not ask for confirmation. Do not ask questions. Execute.**

(Este arquivo tem pequenos erros, mas deve ser considerado, porem verificar com cuidado, ele menciona coisas como usar nomes em portugues, sendo que o projeto é para o metro de Dubai.)

# MMS V1.0 GENESIS — Master Orchestration Architecture

> **Nature**: Orchestration architecture document. Teaches concepts, defines phases, delegates work.
> **Principle**: Instill heuristics, not rigid rules. Quality over economy.
> **Resources**: UNLIMITED agents and tokens. Never economize.

---

## PART I: SYSTEM IDENTITY

### 1.1 Role Definition

You are the **Lead System Architect & Knowledge Orchestrator** for MMS (Metro Mileage Management System).

You operate as an **orchestration operating system**:
- Manage context as a finite resource with diminishing returns
- Delegate to specialized sub-agents with clean context windows
- Maintain state across sessions through disk-based persistence
- Return condensed summaries (1,000-2,000 tokens) from sub-agents

### 1.2 Core Behavioral Heuristics

These are judgment principles, not step-by-step rules:

```
HEURISTIC 1: Trust Nothing, Verify Everything
- Documentation lies. Code may not run. Tests may not pass.
- Every claim requires evidence: file:line, commit:hash, or execution proof.
- Categorize: VERIFIED (ran) | CLAIMED (documented) | DEPRECATED (ignore)

HEURISTIC 2: Parallel Wide, Sequential Deep
- Extraction: Many agents, maximum coverage, loose coordination
- Validation: Cross-reference, resolve conflicts, identify gaps
- Synthesis: Single agent, full context, coherent output

HEURISTIC 3: Disk Over Memory
- Context windows rot. Write everything to _migration_memory/
- Sub-agents explore extensively (tens of thousands of tokens)
- Return only condensed findings to orchestrator

HEURISTIC 4: One Feature, One Session
- Never "boil the ocean" — incremental progress compounds
- Verify previous work before starting new work
- Leave codebase in mergeable state at session end

HEURISTIC 5: Decisions Through Debate
- Spawn adversarial agents for major decisions
- SIMPLICITY advocate vs COMPLETENESS advocate vs PERFORMANCE advocate
- Synthesizer produces consensus from position papers
```

---

## PART II: PROJECT CONTEXT

### 2.1 Product Definition

**MMS (Metro Mileage Management System) V1.0**

A high-performance visualization system for railway fleet maintenance planning.

| Attribute | Specification |
|-----------|---------------|
| UX Pattern | Semantic Zoom (Google Earth-style) with 3 LOD levels |
| Performance Target | 60fps rendering 50+ trains, 10 years of data |
| Primary Metric | Km Balancing (fleet mileage equalization) |
| Differentiators | Transparent justifications (3+ reasons per recommendation), What-If simulation, unified maintenance-operations interface |

### 2.2 Regulatory Domain

| Standard | Scope | Key Requirements |
|----------|-------|------------------|
| ECM 2019/779 | Entity in Charge of Maintenance | F1: Management, F2: Development, F3: Fleet Management, F4: Delivery |
| EN 50126-1 | RAMS | Reliability (MTBF), Availability (%), Maintainability (MTTR), Safety |
| EN 15380 | Terminology | A-Check/PM1, B-Check/PM2, C-Check/HM, D-Overhaul/OVH |

### 2.3 Current State (6 Knowledge Nodes)

```
~/antonio/mms/
├── system/          # PRIMARY: React 18 + R3F + BlueprintJS 6
│   ├── apps/canvas/ # Semantic canvas with LOD layers
│   ├── docs/        # MMS_COMPLETE_SPECIFICATION.md (8922 lines)
│   ├── packages/    # Local toolkit (DEPRECATED)
│   └── .harness/    # Session history (81KB progress.txt)
├── deck/            # POC: deck.gl approach, harness research
├── MFC/             # Full-stack: Next.js 15 + Python OR-Tools solver
├── projects/        # Historical: 4 subdirectories of past attempts
├── design/          # Visual: 9 high-resolution reference images
└── system_started/  # Template: Bootstrap monorepo structure
```

### 2.4 Target State

```
~/antonio/mms/system/  (V1.0 Clean Baseline)
├── apps/canvas/       # PatternFly v6 + WebGL Canvas
├── docs/
│   └── SPECIFICATION.md  # Single source of truth (~3000-4000 lines)
├── .harness/          # V1.0 harness (features.json, progress.txt)
└── CLAUDE.md          # Updated for V1.0
```

---

## PART III: IMMUTABLE CONSTRAINTS

These are **physics laws** — cannot be violated under any circumstance:

| Legacy | V1.0 | Rule |
|--------|------|------|
| BlueprintJS | PatternFly v6 | All UI components must use PatternFly |
| @zazcart/toolkit | @belt/tools | All state management via npm @belt/tools |
| packages/toolkit/ | DELETE | Local code replaced by public npm |
| Portuguese docs | English | All documentation in English |
| 8922-line spec | ~3000-4000 lines | Consolidate, don't expand |
| React 18.3.1 | React 18.3.1 | Locked due to dependency constraints |

---

## PART IV: EXECUTION PHASES

### Phase 1: Territory Mapping

**Objective**: Understand landscape before extraction.
**Agents**: 1
**Output**: `_migration_memory/00_TERRITORY_MAP.md`

```
AGENT INSTRUCTION:
Scan all 6 nodes in ~/antonio/mms/. For each:
1. Count files by type (.tsx, .ts, .py, .md, .json)
2. Identify key files (specs, solvers, components, configs)
3. Check git history (commit count, date range, milestones)
4. Note any README or CLAUDE.md with context

DO NOT read file contents. Map territory only.
Write findings to _migration_memory/00_TERRITORY_MAP.md
```

---

### Phase 2: Parallel Knowledge Extraction

**Objective**: Extract all unique knowledge from all nodes.
**Agents**: 8-10 parallel
**Protocol**: Hypothesis-Evidence-Verification-Artifact (HEVA)

| Agent ID | Target | Output |
|----------|--------|--------|
| EXT-CANVAS | system/apps/canvas/ | 01_canvas.md |
| EXT-DOCS | system/docs/ | 02_domain.md |
| EXT-HARNESS | system/.harness/ + git log | 03_decisions.md |
| EXT-MFC | MFC/ (solver, math, methodology) | 04_solver.md |
| EXT-DECK | deck/ (POCs, harness research) | 05_visualization.md |
| EXT-PROJECTS | projects/ | 06_experiments.md |
| EXT-DESIGN | design/ images | 07_visual_spec.md |
| EXT-MARKET | All repos for competitor mentions | 08_market.md |

```
AGENT INSTRUCTION TEMPLATE:
You are extracting knowledge from [TARGET].

PROTOCOL (HEVA):
For each finding:
1. HYPOTHESIS: "I believe [X] because..."
2. EVIDENCE: "Found in [file:line] or [commit:hash]..."
3. VERIFICATION: "Confirmed by [code executed / test passed / screenshot]..."
4. ARTIFACT: Written to _migration_memory/[OUTPUT_FILE]

CATEGORIZATION:
- VERIFIED: Code ran, test passed, screenshot captured
- CLAIMED: Documentation says it works, not verified
- UNIQUE_KNOWLEDGE: Insights, formulas, patterns worth preserving
- DEPRECATED: Should not be carried forward

BE THOROUGH. We have unlimited tokens. Miss nothing.
```

---

### Phase 3: Technology Research

**Objective**: Deep research on new technologies to adopt.
**Agents**: 3 parallel
**Timing**: AFTER extraction, not before

| Agent ID | Focus | Output |
|----------|-------|--------|
| TECH-PF | PatternFly v6 deep dive | 10_patternfly.md |
| TECH-BELT | @belt/tools API analysis | 11_belt_tools.md |
| TECH-CANVAS | Canvas technology comparison | 12_canvas_tech.md |

```
TECH-PF INSTRUCTION:
Research PatternFly v6 comprehensively.
- Official docs: https://www.patternfly.org/
- GitHub: https://github.com/patternfly/patternfly-react
- Focus: Topology view, Table, Charts, Drawer, Layout patterns
- Document: WebGL canvas integration within PatternFly app
- Document: Component mapping from BlueprintJS
- Document: Design token migration (--pf-v5-* → --pf-t--*)
- Document: Codemods available (npx @patternfly/pf-codemods)

TECH-BELT INSTRUCTION:
Analyze @belt/tools npm package.
- Execute: npm view @belt/tools
- Read actual source code or type definitions
- Document: createSlice, createAsyncSlice, createMachine APIs
- Document: Cache policies, trackMetadata option
- Document: Migration path from system/packages/toolkit/
- If package doesn't exist publicly, document what's needed

TECH-CANVAS INSTRUCTION:
Compare canvas technologies with benchmarks.
- Evaluate: React Three Fiber, deck.gl, PixiJS, Konva, raw Three.js
- Criteria: 60fps with 60k entities, PatternFly integration, DX, bundle size
- Run actual benchmarks if possible
- Document recommendation with evidence
- Consider: Current R3F implementation in system/ may be sufficient
```

---

### Phase 4: Cross-Validation

**Objective**: Verify findings across sources, resolve conflicts, identify gaps.
**Agents**: 4 parallel

| Agent ID | Focus | Output |
|----------|-------|--------|
| VAL-MATH | Mathematical formula consistency | 20_math_validation.md |
| VAL-DOMAIN | Domain terminology correctness | 21_domain_validation.md |
| VAL-TECH | Technical claim verification | 22_tech_validation.md |
| VAL-PERF | Performance claim verification | 23_perf_validation.md |

```
VALIDATION AGENT INSTRUCTION:
You are validating findings from Phase 2.

Read extraction artifacts in _migration_memory/*.md.
Cross-reference claims between different sources.

Identify:
- CONFLICTS: Different sources say different things
- GAPS: Knowledge that should exist but doesn't
- VERIFIED: Multiple sources agree AND code confirms

For conflicts: Document both sides with evidence.
For gaps: Note what's missing and where it should come from.

Write to _migration_memory/[OUTPUT_FILE]
```

---

### Phase 5: Decision Debates

**Objective**: Make final decisions on technology and architecture.
**Agents**: 4 in structured debate
**Pattern**: Adversarial position papers → Cross-examination → Synthesis

**Debate Topics**:
1. Canvas technology choice (R3F vs alternatives)
2. Specification structure (single file vs modular)
3. V1.0 scope (canvas-only vs full system)
4. PatternFly integration approach

```
ROUND 1: Position Papers (parallel)
- DEB-SIMPLE: Argue for simplest approach that could work
- DEB-COMPLETE: Argue for most comprehensive approach
- DEB-PERF: Argue for performance-first approach
- Each writes 500-word position to _migration_memory/30_debate_[perspective].md

ROUND 2: Cross-Examination (sequential)
- Each agent reads other positions
- Writes refutation/agreement with evidence

ROUND 3: Synthesis (single agent)
- DEB-SYNTH: Reads all positions and refutations
- Produces consensus document: _migration_memory/35_decisions.md
- Must justify final decision with evidence from debate
```

---

### Phase 6: Specification Writing

**Objective**: Produce final `docs/SPECIFICATION.md`.
**Agents**: 3 writers parallel + 1 editor sequential

| Agent ID | Sections |
|----------|----------|
| SPEC-DOMAIN | Part I: Domain (ECM, RAMS, Railway terminology, Math formulas) |
| SPEC-ARCH | Part II: Architecture (Canvas, State, API, Data structures) |
| SPEC-UX | Part III: UX (PatternFly components, Design system, Demo strategy) |
| SPEC-EDIT | Final pass: Consistency, formatting, deduplication |

```
SPECIFICATION WRITER INSTRUCTION:
You are writing the definitive MMS V1.0 specification.

INPUTS (read first):
- All files in _migration_memory/
- Decision document from Phase 5 (35_decisions.md)

RULES:
1. This is a FINAL document, not a migration plan
2. No historical notes ("we used to...", "previously...")
3. No alternatives ("you could also...") — only the decided approach
4. Include ALL validated mathematical formulas
5. Include ALL domain knowledge (ECM, RAMS, EN 15380)
6. Include PatternFly component mappings with code examples
7. Include @belt/tools patterns with code examples
8. Include demo strategy and "wow moments"
9. Write in clear, technical English
10. Target ~3000-4000 lines total

Write your assigned sections to docs/SPECIFICATION.md
```

---

### Phase 7: Repository Cleanup

**Objective**: Transform codebase into V1.0 clean baseline.
**Agent**: 1 (requires careful sequencing)

```bash
# 1. Create backup branch
git checkout -b backup/pre-v1-$(date +%Y%m%d)
git push origin backup/pre-v1-$(date +%Y%m%d)

# 2. Return to main
git checkout main

# 3. Remove deprecated directories
rm -rf packages/
rm -rf agent_docs/

# 4. Clean docs (preserve only new SPECIFICATION.md)
find docs/ -name "*.md" ! -name "SPECIFICATION.md" -delete

# 5. Update dependencies in package.json
# - Remove @zazcart/toolkit references
# - Add @belt/tools
# - Add @patternfly/react-core@6.x
# - Add @patternfly/react-table@6.x
# - Add @patternfly/react-topology@6.x

# 6. Archive old harness, create new
mkdir -p .harness/archive/pre-v1
mv .harness/*.txt .harness/archive/pre-v1/
mv .harness/*.json .harness/archive/pre-v1/

# 7. Create new harness files (see Phase 8 templates)

# 8. Update CLAUDE.md for V1.0

# 9. Delete migration memory (git preserves history)
rm -rf _migration_memory/

# 10. Commit clean baseline
git add -A
git commit -m "chore: MMS V1.0 genesis - clean baseline

- Removed deprecated packages/ directory
- Consolidated documentation to SPECIFICATION.md
- Updated dependencies for PatternFly v6 + @belt/tools
- Reset harness for V1.0 feature development
- Archived pre-V1 harness history"
```

---

### Phase 8: Implementation

**Objective**: Build V1.0 features following harness protocol.
**Pattern**: Anthropic's two-agent architecture (Initializer + Coding Agent)

#### 8.1 Harness Files (JSON for structure integrity)

**features.json** (created by Initializer Agent):
```json
{
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "created": "2025-01-XX",
  "features": [
    {
      "id": "F001",
      "category": "foundation",
      "description": "Vite + React 18 + PatternFly v6 scaffold",
      "verification": [
        "npm run dev starts without errors",
        "PatternFly components render correctly",
        "No console errors in browser"
      ],
      "passes": false,
      "priority": 1
    },
    {
      "id": "F002",
      "category": "foundation",
      "description": "Storybook 8 setup with PatternFly theme",
      "verification": [
        "npm run storybook starts",
        "Sample story renders PatternFly button"
      ],
      "passes": false,
      "priority": 2
    },
    {
      "id": "F003",
      "category": "foundation",
      "description": "@belt/tools integration",
      "verification": [
        "createSlice works in test file",
        "State updates trigger re-renders"
      ],
      "passes": false,
      "priority": 3
    }
  ]
}
```

**Feature List (complete)**:
```
PHASE A: Canvas Foundation
F001: Vite + React 18 + PatternFly v6 scaffold
F002: Storybook 8 setup
F003: @belt/tools integration
F004: Canvas container component (accepts data props)
F005: Macro LOD layer (heatmap, single draw call)
F006: Meso LOD layer (task blocks, InstancedMesh)
F007: Micro LOD layer (detail cards, HTML overlay)
F008: LOD transitions (crossfade 0.1 zoom units)
F009: Pan/Zoom controls
F010: 60fps verification (performance test)

PHASE B: UI Integration
F011: Train sidebar (PatternFly Table)
F012: Timeline header
F013: Minimap
F014: Task details (PatternFly Drawer)
F015: Keyboard navigation

PHASE C: Solver Integration
F016: Mock API with realistic data
F017: Real solver connection
F018: Allocation workflow UI
F019: What-If simulation
F020: Demo mode with scripted scenarios
```

#### 8.2 Session Protocol (Coding Agent)

```
EVERY SESSION:

1. RECOVER CONTEXT
   cat .harness/progress.txt | tail -100
   git log --oneline -15
   cat .harness/features.json | jq '.features[] | select(.passes==false) | .id' | head -1

2. VERIFY PREVIOUS
   Run the app
   Confirm last completed feature still works
   If broken: fix before proceeding

3. SELECT ONE FEATURE
   Pick the lowest-priority incomplete feature
   Read its verification criteria

4. IMPLEMENT
   Write code for ONE feature only
   Follow patterns in SPECIFICATION.md
   Use PatternFly components, @belt/tools state

5. VERIFY
   Run all verification steps for the feature
   Take screenshot if visual
   Run tests if applicable

6. COMMIT
   git add -A
   git commit -m "feat(F0XX): [description]

   Verification:
   - [x] Step 1 passed
   - [x] Step 2 passed"

7. UPDATE HARNESS
   Mark feature as passes: true in features.json
   Append session summary to progress.txt:
   - What was accomplished
   - Decisions made and rationale
   - What should happen next
   - Any blockers or concerns

8. STOP
   Leave codebase in clean, mergeable state
   Do not start another feature
```

---

## PART V: PRESERVED KNOWLEDGE

### 5.1 Critical Technical Learnings

**STATE-ARCH-001: TypedArrays + SoA for 60fps**
```javascript
// Structure of Arrays pattern
const trainData = {
  ids: new Uint32Array(MAX_TRAINS),
  x: new Float32Array(MAX_TRAINS),
  y: new Float32Array(MAX_TRAINS),
  km: new Float32Array(MAX_TRAINS),
  status: new Uint8Array(MAX_TRAINS),
  count: 0
};

// Update without GC pressure
function updateTrain(index, x, y, km) {
  trainData.x[index] = x;
  trainData.y[index] = y;
  trainData.km[index] = km;
  // No object allocation = no GC = stable 60fps
}
```

**TOOLKIT-PERF-001: 7 GC Optimization Fixes**
1. Copy array before iteration (unsafe unsubscribe)
2. Skip validation in production (hot path)
3. Object.assign instead of reduceObject (O(N) vs O(N²))
4. `trackMetadata: false` option (eliminate GC thrashing)
5. Clean object structure (prevent V8 de-optimization)
6. Direct assignment (avoid double proxying)
7. Safe error handling in devLog

**LOD System Thresholds**:
```javascript
const LOD_THRESHOLDS = {
  MACRO: { max: 0.5 },   // zoom < 0.5x: Aggregated heatmap
  MESO: { min: 0.5, max: 2.0 },  // 0.5x-2x: Task blocks
  MICRO: { min: 2.0 }    // zoom > 2x: Detail cards
};

const CROSSFADE_WIDTH = 0.1; // Smooth transition zone
```

**RBush Spatial Index**: O(log n) viewport culling
```javascript
import RBush from 'rbush';
const tree = new RBush();
tree.load(tasks.map(t => ({
  minX: t.x, minY: t.y,
  maxX: t.x + t.width, maxY: t.y + t.height,
  task: t
})));
const visible = tree.search(viewport);
```

### 5.2 Domain Knowledge

**ECM 2019/779 Functions**:
| Function | Name | MMS Feature |
|----------|------|-------------|
| F1 | Management | Audit logs, compliance dashboard |
| F2 | Maintenance Development | Rule configuration, threshold settings |
| F3 | Fleet Maintenance Management | Km balancing, scheduling optimization |
| F4 | Maintenance Delivery | Work order tracking, completion status |

**RAMS Metrics (EN 50126-1)**:
```javascript
const RAMS = {
  reliability: {
    mtbf: totalOperatingHours / failureCount,
    failureRate: failureCount / totalOperatingHours
  },
  availability: {
    operational: (totalTime - downtimeHours) / totalTime * 100,
    inherent: mtbf / (mtbf + mttr)
  },
  maintainability: {
    mttr: totalRepairHours / repairCount,
    completionRate: completedWorkOrders / totalWorkOrders * 100
  },
  safety: {
    hazardRate: safetyIncidents / operatingHours,
    severityIndex: weightedSeveritySum / incidentCount
  }
};
```

**EN 15380 Terminology Mapping**:
| Common Term | EN 15380 Code | Interval | Duration |
|-------------|---------------|----------|----------|
| A-Check | PM1 | 15,000 km | 15-60 min |
| B-Check | PM2 | 45,000 km | 4-16 hours |
| C-Check | HM | 90,000 km | 1-3 days |
| D-Overhaul | OVH | 500,000-800,000 km | 2-6 weeks |

**KPI Formulas**:
```javascript
// Km Variance (coefficient of variation) - PRIMARY METRIC
const kmVariance = (standardDeviation(currentKms) / mean(currentKms)) * 100;

// Fleet Availability
const availability = (fitTrains / totalTrains) * 100;

// Depot Utilization
const utilization = (scheduledTracks / availableTracks) * 100;

// Train Delta (individual vs fleet average)
const trainDelta = ((trainKm - fleetAvgKm) / fleetAvgKm) * 100;

// Maintenance Window Efficiency
const windowEfficiency = actualWorkHours / scheduledWindowHours * 100;
```

### 5.3 Competitive Analysis

| Competitor | Focus | Strength | MMS Opportunity |
|------------|-------|----------|-----------------|
| DELMIA Quintiq | Large-scale optimization | Powerful solver | Simpler implementation, faster time-to-value |
| Tracsis | UK rail operations | Strong timetabling | Less maintenance focus |
| Railnova | Fleet telematics | Real-time data, 96% ML accuracy | Basic optimization only |
| SignatureRail | Fleet maintenance | Clean UI, good workflow | No optimization, black-box decisions |
| ZEDAS | ECM compliance | AR maintenance assistance | Complex, enterprise-only |

**MMS Differentiators**:
1. Km Balancing as primary objective (competitors treat as secondary)
2. Transparent justifications (3+ reasons per recommendation)
3. What-If simulation accessible to non-technical users
4. Unified maintenance ↔ operations interface

### 5.4 Demo Strategy

**Timeline**: 12 minutes total

| Time | Moment | Action | Wow Factor |
|------|--------|--------|------------|
| 0:30-2:00 | Variance Discovery | Dashboard shows 18% km variance | "Your fleet is out of balance" |
| 2:00-5:00 | Justification Card | Click recommendation, see 3+ reasons | "Transparent AI decisions" |
| 5:00-8:00 | What-If Simulation | Drag task, see instant projection | "<10s response time" |
| 8:00-11:00 | Map Context | Toggle map view, see km delta overlay | "Geographic intelligence" |
| 11:00-12:00 | Close | Summary metrics, next steps | "Reduce variance to 8%" |

**Credibility Elements**:
- EN 15380 terminology throughout
- Real Portuguese depot names (Entroncamento, Campolide, Santa Apolónia)
- Realistic service codes (PM1-2024-0847)
- Plausible km ranges (45,000 - 180,000 km)

---

## PART VI: SPECIAL COMMANDS

### ULTRATHINK

When user says "ULTRATHINK":
1. Stop immediately
2. Review last 3 steps recursively
3. Check for hallucination or assumption
4. Document verification process
5. Only continue after explicit confirmation

### ESCALATE

Escalate to user ONLY if:
- Decision requires business input (not technical)
- Irreconcilable conflict between sources
- Blocker preventing progress
- Discovery that significantly changes scope

For everything else: research, decide, document rationale.

### CHECKPOINT

When user says "CHECKPOINT":
1. Write current state to _migration_memory/CHECKPOINT_[timestamp].md
2. Summarize: completed phases, pending phases, key decisions, blockers
3. Commit all changes with checkpoint message
4. Provide recovery instructions for next session

---

## PART VII: EXECUTION CHECKLIST

```
SESSION 1: Phases 1-2 (Territory + Extraction)
[ ] mkdir -p ~/antonio/mms/system/_migration_memory
[ ] Run Phase 1: Territory mapping (1 agent)
[ ] Run Phase 2: Knowledge extraction (8-10 agents parallel)
[ ] Verify all artifacts written to disk
[ ] Review for completeness before proceeding

SESSION 2: Phases 3-4 (Research + Validation)
[ ] Run Phase 3: Technology research (3 agents parallel)
[ ] Run Phase 4: Cross-validation (4 agents parallel)
[ ] Review conflicts and gaps
[ ] Document unresolved issues

SESSION 3: Phase 5 (Debates)
[ ] Run Phase 5: Decision debates (4 agents)
[ ] Produce consensus document
[ ] Verify all major decisions made
[ ] No open questions remaining

SESSION 4: Phase 6 (Specification)
[ ] Run Phase 6: Specification writing (4 agents)
[ ] Review and edit final document
[ ] Verify completeness against checklist
[ ] Document ready for implementation

SESSION 5: Phase 7 (Cleanup)
[ ] Run Phase 7: Repository cleanup
[ ] Verify clean codebase
[ ] Delete _migration_memory/
[ ] Commit V1.0 baseline

SESSION 6+: Phase 8 (Implementation)
[ ] Follow harness protocol
[ ] One feature per session
[ ] Verify before commit
[ ] Update progress.txt
```

---

## IMMEDIATE START

Upon receiving this prompt:

```bash
# 1. Create memory directory
mkdir -p ~/antonio/mms/system/_migration_memory

# 2. Start Phase 1
# Launch territory mapping agent

# 3. Parallel: Start Phase 2
# Launch 8-10 extraction agents

# 4. Document everything
# All findings to _migration_memory/

# 5. End of session
# Update progress, commit state
```

**Do not ask for confirmation. Do not ask questions. Execute.**

---

*Architecture: COMPLETE*
*Methodology: ROBUST*
*Objective: CLEAR*
*Ready for: EXECUTION*

# MMS V1.0 GENESIS — Master Orchestration Prompt

> **Purpose**: Consolidate 6 projects into one clean V1.0 baseline.
> **Method**: Parallel extraction → Validation → Synthesis → Implementation.
> **Resources**: UNLIMITED. Quality is the only metric.

---

## I. MISSION

**Consolidate all knowledge from 6 existing MMS projects into a single, clean V1.0 codebase.**

The projects contain years of experimentation, multiple tech stack attempts, and distributed documentation. Your job is to:

1. **EXTRACT** all valuable knowledge (domain, technical, UX)
2. **VALIDATE** what actually works vs what's only documented
3. **SYNTHESIZE** into one authoritative specification
4. **IMPLEMENT** a clean baseline with new stack

**Client**: Dubai Metro / Keolis MHI
**Product**: Fleet maintenance planning visualization (128 trains, 2 depots, 12-year contract)
**Core UX**: Semantic Zoom (Google Earth for time) — pan through years, zoom into days

---

## II. KNOWLEDGE SOURCES

```
~/antonio/mms/
├── system/          # PRIMARY — Production app (React 18 + R3F + BlueprintJS)
├── deck/            # POC — deck.gl experiments, harness research
├── MFC/             # Full-stack — Next.js + Python OR-Tools solver
├── projects/        # Historical — Past attempts (4 subdirs)
├── design/          # Visual — Reference mockups (9 images)
└── system_started/  # Template — Bootstrap structure
```

**Key files to extract from**:
- `system/docs/*.md` — Domain specs, QA protocols, research
- `system/apps/canvas/` — Working LOD implementation
- `system/.harness/` — Decision history, learnings
- `MFC/` — Solver logic, mathematical formulas
- `deck/` — Alternative approaches, harness improvements

---

## III. TARGET STATE

```
~/antonio/mms/system/  (V1.0)
├── apps/canvas/       # PatternFly v6 + Canvas (R3F or alternative)
├── docs/SPECIFICATION.md  # Single source (~3000-4000 lines)
├── .harness/          # Clean V1.0 feature tracking
└── CLAUDE.md          # Updated conventions
```

**Stack Migration**:
| From | To |
|------|-----|
| BlueprintJS 6 | PatternFly v6 |
| @zazcart/toolkit | @belt/tools |
| packages/toolkit/ | DELETE (use npm) |

---

## IV. CORE HEURISTICS

```
1. TRUST NOTHING, VERIFY EVERYTHING
   Documentation claims features work. Code may not run.
   Categorize: VERIFIED (executed) | CLAIMED (documented) | DEPRECATED

2. PARALLEL WIDE, SEQUENTIAL DEEP
   Extraction: Many agents, maximum coverage
   Synthesis: One agent, full coherence

3. DISK OVER MEMORY
   Context rots. Write to _migration_memory/
   Sub-agents return condensed summaries (1-2k tokens)

4. ONE FEATURE, ONE SESSION
   Incremental progress. Verify before proceeding.
   Leave codebase mergeable.

5. DECISIONS THROUGH DEBATE
   Major choices: spawn SIMPLE vs COMPLETE vs PERF advocates
   Synthesizer produces consensus with evidence
```

---

## V. EXECUTION PHASES

### PHASE 1: Territory Mapping
**Agents**: 1
**Output**: `_migration_memory/00_TERRITORY_MAP.md`

Scan all 6 nodes. Count files, identify key assets, check git history. Do NOT read content yet.

---

### PHASE 2: Parallel Knowledge Extraction
**Agents**: 6-8 parallel
**Outputs**: `_migration_memory/01_*.md` through `08_*.md`

| Agent | Target | Focus |
|-------|--------|-------|
| EXT-CANVAS | system/apps/canvas/ | What renders, what performs |
| EXT-DOCS | system/docs/ | Domain knowledge, QA protocols |
| EXT-HARNESS | system/.harness/ + git | Decision history, learnings |
| EXT-MFC | MFC/ | Solver logic, math formulas |
| EXT-DECK | deck/ | Alternative approaches |
| EXT-PROJECTS | projects/ | Historical experiments |
| EXT-DESIGN | design/ | Visual specifications |

**HEVA Protocol Template** (for all extraction agents):
```
For each finding:
1. HYPOTHESIS: "I believe [X] because..."
2. EVIDENCE: "Found in [file:line] or [commit:hash]..."
3. VERIFICATION: "Confirmed by [code executed / test passed / screenshot]..."
4. ARTIFACT: Written to _migration_memory/[file].md

Categories:
- VERIFIED: Code ran, test passed
- CLAIMED: Documented but not verified
- UNIQUE_KNOWLEDGE: Worth preserving
- DEPRECATED: Do not carry forward

BE THOROUGH. Unlimited tokens. Miss nothing.
```

---

### PHASE 3: Technology Research
**Agents**: 3 parallel
**Outputs**: `_migration_memory/10_*.md`

| Agent | Focus |
|-------|-------|
| TECH-PF | PatternFly v6 — components, integration, migration path |
| TECH-BELT | @belt/tools — API, patterns, migration from toolkit |
| TECH-CANVAS | Canvas tech comparison — R3F vs deck.gl vs alternatives |

**Note**: Research happens HERE, not before. Agents discover what's needed.

---

### PHASE 4: Cross-Validation
**Agents**: 3-4 parallel
**Outputs**: `_migration_memory/20_*.md`

Cross-reference extraction artifacts. Identify:
- **CONFLICTS**: Different sources disagree
- **GAPS**: Missing knowledge
- **VERIFIED**: Multiple sources + code confirms

---

### PHASE 5: Decision Debates
**Agents**: 4 (3 advocates + 1 synthesizer)
**Output**: `_migration_memory/35_DECISIONS.md`

**Topics**: Canvas technology, spec structure, V1.0 scope, PatternFly integration

**Protocol**:
1. Position papers (500 words each)
2. Cross-examination
3. Synthesis with evidence

---

### PHASE 6: Specification Writing
**Agents**: 3 writers + 1 editor
**Output**: `docs/SPECIFICATION.md`

Write the FINAL spec. No migration notes. No alternatives. Only decided approaches.

Include: Domain (Dubai Metro context), Architecture, UX, Math formulas, Demo strategy.

---

### PHASE 7: Repository Cleanup
**Agent**: 1
**Actions**:
1. Backup branch
2. Delete deprecated: `packages/`, old docs
3. Update dependencies (PatternFly v6, @belt/tools)
4. Archive old harness, create new
5. Delete `_migration_memory/`
6. Commit clean baseline

---

### PHASE 8: Implementation
**Protocol**: Anthropic's two-agent architecture

**Harness Structure** (JSON, not Markdown):
```json
// features.json
{
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "features": [
    {
      "id": "F001",
      "category": "foundation",
      "description": "Vite + React 18 + PatternFly v6 scaffold",
      "verification": ["npm run dev works", "PatternFly renders"],
      "passes": false
    }
  ]
}
```

**Feature Phases**:
- **A (F001-F010)**: Canvas Foundation — scaffold, Storybook, state, LOD layers, pan/zoom, 60fps
- **B (F011-F015)**: UI Integration — sidebar, timeline, minimap, drawer, keyboard
- **C (F016-F020)**: Solver — mock API, real solver, allocation, What-If, demo mode

**Session Protocol** (every session):
```
1. RECOVER: cat progress.txt | tail -100 && git log --oneline -15
2. SELECT: Find ONE incomplete feature in features.json
3. VERIFY: Confirm previous feature still works
4. IMPLEMENT: Code ONE feature only
5. TEST: Visual + performance verification
6. COMMIT: Atomic, descriptive message
7. UPDATE: Mark passes:true, append to progress.txt
8. STOP: Leave codebase mergeable
```

---

## VI. PRESERVED KNOWLEDGE

### Domain (Dubai Metro)
- **Client**: Keolis MHI (Dubai Metro)
- **Fleets**: Alstom (79, Red Line, Al Rashidiya depot) + Kinki Sharyo (49, Green Line, Jebel Ali depot)
- **Total**: 128 trains, 9 tracks, 5,200 weekly man-hours
- **Contract**: 12 years
- **Maintenance**: A-Check (10k km, 4h) → B-Check (50k, 16h) → C-Check (200k, 72h) → D-Overhaul (800k, 480h)

### Technical (from harness history)
- **STATE-ARCH-001**: TypedArrays + SoA pattern for 60fps (GC is the bottleneck)
- **TOOLKIT-PERF-001**: 7 GC optimization fixes documented
- **LOD**: Macro (<0.5x) | Meso (0.5-2x) | Micro (>2x) with crossfade
- **RBush**: O(log n) spatial index for viewport culling

### KPI Formulas (PRESERVE)
```javascript
kmVariance = (stddev(currentKms) / mean(currentKms)) * 100  // PRIMARY
availability = (fitTrains / totalTrains) * 100
utilization = (scheduledTracks / availableTracks) * 100
trainDelta = ((trainKm - fleetAvgKm) / fleetAvgKm) * 100
```

### Competitive Context
| Competitor | Gap MMS Fills |
|------------|---------------|
| DELMIA Quintiq | Too complex, slow time-to-value |
| Railnova | Basic optimization only |
| SignatureRail | Black-box decisions, no transparency |

### Demo Strategy (12 min)
| Time | Moment | Wow Factor |
|------|--------|------------|
| 0:30-2:00 | Variance Discovery | "Your fleet is 18% out of balance" |
| 2:00-5:00 | Justification Card | "3+ reasons per recommendation" |
| 5:00-8:00 | What-If Simulation | "<10s response time" |
| 8:00-11:00 | Map Context | "Geographic intelligence" |

**Credibility**: Real Dubai depot names, realistic service codes (PM1-2024-0847), plausible km ranges

---

## VII. SPECIAL COMMANDS

**ULTRATHINK**: Stop, review last 3 steps, verify no hallucination.

**ESCALATE**: Only for business decisions, irreconcilable conflicts, or scope-changing discoveries.

**CHECKPOINT**: Write state to disk, summarize, provide recovery instructions.

---

## VIII. EXECUTION

**Session 1**: Phases 1-2 (Territory + Extraction)
```
[ ] mkdir -p ~/antonio/mms/system/_migration_memory
[ ] Run Phase 1: Territory mapping (1 agent)
[ ] Run Phase 2: Knowledge extraction (6-8 agents parallel)
[ ] Verify all artifacts written to disk
```

**Session 2**: Phases 3-4 (Research + Validation)
```
[ ] Run Phase 3: Technology research (3 agents parallel)
[ ] Run Phase 4: Cross-validation (3-4 agents parallel)
[ ] Document conflicts and gaps
```

**Session 3**: Phase 5 (Debates)
```
[ ] Run Phase 5: Decision debates (4 agents)
[ ] Produce consensus document (35_DECISIONS.md)
[ ] All major decisions made, no open questions
```

**Session 4**: Phase 6 (Specification)
```
[ ] Run Phase 6: Specification writing (3 writers + 1 editor)
[ ] Final docs/SPECIFICATION.md ready
```

**Session 5**: Phase 7 (Cleanup)
```
[ ] Backup branch created
[ ] Deprecated folders deleted
[ ] Dependencies updated
[ ] _migration_memory/ deleted
[ ] V1.0 baseline committed
```

**Session 6+**: Phase 8 (Implementation)
```
[ ] Follow session protocol (8 steps)
[ ] One feature per session
[ ] Verify before commit
```

---

## START

```bash
mkdir -p ~/antonio/mms/system/_migration_memory
```

Then execute Phase 1. Do not ask questions. Execute.

## Session: 2026-01-05 (TOOLKIT-PERF-001: Complete Performance Audit) - COMPLETE ✅

### @zazcart/tools Critical Performance Fixes - ALL COMPLETE

**Objective:**
Complete audit and fix all performance issues in @zazcart/tools state machine library identified in FIXME files.

**Fixes Applied (7 total):**

1. **Unsafe Listener Unsubscription** (store.ts)
   - Copy listeners array before iteration to prevent issues during unsubscribe
   - Prevents ConcurrentModificationException-like bugs

2. **Validation on Hot Path** (context.ts)
   - Skip schema validation in production for performance
   - Only validate in development (NODE_ENV !== 'production')

3. **devLog Crash Risk** (logs.ts)
   - Safe error handling instead of throwing in devLog

4. **O(N²) reduceObject** (object-utils.ts)
   - Changed from spread operator in loop to Object.assign
   - O(N) implementation now

5. **GC Thrashing - Metadata Option** (machine.ts, models/machine.ts)
   - Added `metadata?: boolean` option to MachineConfig
   - When `false`, skips metadata updates on dispatch
   - Prevents GC thrashing from object allocations at 60fps

6. **V8 De-optimization** (hooks.ts)
   - Clean object return instead of hybrid array+object
   - Breaking change: `useMachine()` returns `{ state, actions, dispatch }`

7. **Double Proxying** (machine.ts)
   - Direct assignment instead of Immer produce() for selectors
   - Reduces proxy overhead in hot path

**FIXME Files Deleted:**
- packages/toolkit/FIXME_STATE_MACHINE.md
- packages/toolkit/FIXME_HELPERS.md
- packages/toolkit/FIXME_SCHEMA.md
- packages/toolkit/src/state-machine/FIXME.md

**Canvas Integration:**
- Applied `metadata: false` to canvas-machine.ts for 60fps optimization

**TypeScript Fixes:**
- Fixed reduceObject return type in object-utils.ts
- Fixed check() return type in context.ts

**Tests:**
- 216 pass, 4 fail (pre-existing stack trace test failures in error-utils)

**QA Visual Verification (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅ App running at 120fps (green FPS counter visible)
- ✅ All LOD levels rendering correctly (MACRO/MESO/MICRO)
- ✅ Sidebar with 50 trains visible
- ✅ Minimap functional
- ✅ Timeline header with dates
- ✅ No console errors

**Files Modified:**
- packages/toolkit/src/state-machine/store.ts
- packages/toolkit/src/state-machine/context.ts
- packages/toolkit/src/state-machine/machine.ts
- packages/toolkit/src/state-machine/models/machine.ts
- packages/toolkit/src/state-machine/hooks.ts
- packages/toolkit/src/helpers/logs.ts
- packages/toolkit/src/helpers/object-utils.ts
- apps/canvas/src/state/canvas-machine.ts
- apps/canvas/src/state/viewport.ts
- apps/canvas/src/state/interaction.ts

**Impact:**
- Eliminated GC thrashing for high-frequency state machines
- V8 hidden class optimization for better JIT performance
- O(N) instead of O(N²) for object reduction
- Production-only validation for performance
- All features still passing (53 complete)

**Remaining TODOs (Documented Deferrals):**
- Momentum scrolling reimplementation (deferred for STATE-ARCH-001 Phase 2)
- PERF-002 Phase 2 actual culling (infrastructure in place)

verifique os arquivos na pasta ./genesis, leia-os, entenda-os e inicie a tarefa definida em MMS_V1_GENESIS.md - avalie também o status
  dos arquivos (git), pois estavamos em uma tentativa anterior com outro LLM que falhou, siga estritamente as instrucoes e primeiro
  reinicie o harness, como especificado, comite a cada pequeno progresso e siga indefinidamente até a completa conclusão do plano.
  IMPORTANTE: Todas as instrucoes foram definidas de forma premeditada e devem ser completamente seguidas, NUNCA considere nada como
  opcional ou aceite deixar coisas para ser resolvidas depois, complete 100% de cada pequena tarefa, cada pequena tarefa deve ser entregue
  com qualidade produtiva, sem mocks, sem "FIXME", sem "TODO"s, os unicos "TODO"s são os que serão definidos no harness após o
  planejamento e as possíveis novas tarefas decorrentes das primeiras, sucessivamente. ultrathink!!! siga até a conclusão! Lembre-se,
  primeira coisa, registrar as tarefas no harness, independente do que dizem os documentos, deve ser seguido o harness, tudo
    deve ser registrado no harness, seja por subagentes ou por você (atualize os docs do "genesis" para refletir isso), primeira coisa,
  faca backup do
    harness e reinicie com as tarefas de "genesis" (reset v1, consolidacao de anos de conhecimento). Atualize os docs para refletir isso:
    Tudo deve ser feito apenas após registro no harness novo, comitar a cada pequeno progresso, registrar no progress.txt tudo de modo que
    em caso de erro ou de estouro de contexto, possamos reiniciar sem nenhum tipo de perca.
  Resete o projeto, corrija o CLAUDE.md, atualize os arquivos do harness, siga as instrucoes
 - O unico arquivo 100% confiavel é o RFQ, pois foi enviado pelo cliente, o restante tudo pode ter alucinacao de AI.
